title: AST DUI: Fairbanks, AK [AK25085317]
summary: On August 23, 2025, The Alaska State Troopers received a REDDI report that led to the arrest of Brent Strickland for driving under the influence in the Badger area. Strickland, who refused to perform field sobriety tests, was taken into custody after a traffic stop where officers observed signs of intoxication. At the Trooper Post, a breath test revealed a blood alcohol content of .206, and he was subsequently remanded to the Fairbanks Correctional Center.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

summary: On August 22, 2025, at approximately 1520 hours, Alaska state Troopers responded to a Motor Vehicle Collision on Badger Road near North Pole, Alaska. Troopers responded to the scene of the accident and initiated an investigation. The investigation revealed that K.Y. (15) of Fairbanks was driving in....

title: AST DUI: Fairbanks [AK25085078]
summary: On August 22, 2025, at approximately 2139 hours, Alaska State Troopers received a call about a vehicle in a ditch in Ester. Upon arrival and subsequent investigation, Troopers found Zoe Rice (24) of Fairbanks, Alaska, to be operating a motor vehicle while under the influence of an alcoholic beverage. Emergency Medical Services were requested out to the scene to render aid and transport Zoe Rice. The Charges of DUI are being forwarded to the District Attorney's office.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Problems with Prince Rupert, British Columbia, as a ferry destination make Hyder a better location
summary: This road connects Hyder, Alaska, with its seaplane base, in September 2018. (Photo by Bun Lee/Getty Images) Southeast Alaska has not had any ferry service to the mainland road system in British Columbia since 2022 and for the five years prior to that, what it had was unreliable and spotty. This was despite some Alaskans voicing a strong preference for a ferry to Prince Rupert, B.C., over Bellingham, Washingt...

title: AST Illegal Dumping: MP1303 AK Hwy [AK25084833]
summary: On 8/22/25, at 1312 hours, the Tok VPSO was contacted about a dump site on Tetlin Native Corporation lands. Investigation was able to identify a suspect in this matter. The investigation in ongoing and charges are pending.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

They are also available on the My Koniag Shareholder portal at mykoniag.koniag.com. These important documents provide a comprehen...

title: Alaska medical board seeks to restrict abortion, transgender medical care
summary: The offices of the Alaska Department of Health and Social Services are seen in Juneau on Friday, July 1, 2022. (Photo by Lisa Phu/Alaska Beacon) The politically appointed board that regulates health care in Alaska voted unanimously Friday to recommend that the state restrict medical care for transgender youth in the state and approved a letter asking Alaska lawmakers to end access to abortion in the late stages of pregnancy. On transgender care, the board approved a draft regulation that — if made final — would declare that providing gender transition...

title: Last thoughts on salmon and steelhead extinction in the Pacific Northwest
summary: In this undated photo, Chinook salmon spawn in Snake River. (Photo by Natalie Fobes/Getty Images) Editor’s note: This is the fourth installment of a four-part series on salmon. Part One described the emergency effort to rescue Washington state’s last population of Snake River spring and summer Chinook salmon, in the Tucannon River. Part Two explored salmon and steelhead extinction threshold science, showing the Pacific Northwest is entering extinction momentum that will be very difficult to escape. Part Three explored the good work that has restored the Grande Ronde and Imnaha river areas, more so than any other salmon landscape in the Snake River Basin. I conclude here this four-part reflection on salmon and steelhead extinction science, and extinction’s approach and effects for rivers and people in Washington and Oregon’s Snake River tributaries. The last words go to many I interviewed, after a few more from me. I thank everyone who talked with me: Amy Jayne, Bobby Fossek, John Milbe...

title: Discussion of sale of Mosquito Lake School continues, next stop is Planning Commission
summary: The Haines Planning Commission plans to discuss dividing the Mosquito Lake School Facility into multiple parcels. That’s because the Haines Borough Assembly is considering selling all or part of the land to help maintain a healthy budget. KHNS has this story about balancing community needs and finan...
